title: The Overflowing Reservoir
subtitle: A visualisation for anchoring money as a continuously refilling resource
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation for building a felt sense of money as something that replenishes rather than depletes. The image is a reservoir that fills as it empties — water arriving at the same rate it leaves, keeping the level steady and rising.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 61 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796). The replenishment visualisation is an original extension of the source material.

practice.whenToUse: Use on mornings when the background hum of scarcity is up, or when you want to anchor a felt sense of continuous supply before the day's spending begins.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation for building a felt sense of replenishment. The image anchors the idea that money leaving does not reduce the available supply — that the reservoir refills as it empties, keeping the level steady and, over time, rising.

A large stone reservoir filled with clean, cool water. Water flows in from a spring at one end and out through a channel at the other. The level stays constant. As you watch, the inflow increases slightly — the level rises an inch, then another. The outflow continues unchanged. The reservoir is full and growing fuller.
Sit comfortably with your eyes closed. Take three slow breaths, one after another, before you begin.
Picture the reservoir. It is large — wider than you can see from one end to the other. The stone is old and solid. The water is clear. You can see the bottom in the shallows near the edge.
Find the spring at the far end. Watch the water arriving: a steady, unhurried flow. It doesn't rush. It doesn't stop. It arrives at the same pace, day and night, whether you watch it or not.
Find the outflow channel. Water moves through it — payments made, money spent, the ordinary business of a life. The channel is open. You are not blocking it. The water moves through without drama.

Check the water level. The spring is faster than the channel. The reservoir is filling. Watch it for a minute — the surface rising slowly, catching the light, perfectly still except for the motion of the current.
This is your money: in, out, steady, and rising. You do not need to guard the channel. You do not need to block the outflow. The spring keeps coming.
Open your eyes when you're ready. If you want to keep the image, write one line about what the reservoir looked like. The image stays more easily when it has a specific detail attached to it.
Adapted from the Day 61 replenishment theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
